pilot_signal_strength.evidence: Budget equivalence confirmed (0.21% spread across 4 methods). BEM differentiates WD budgets (0.00-0.90). CWD does NOT beat baseline, supporting core thesis.

hypothesis_survival.evidence: H3 strongly supported, H4 preliminary support, H1 noisy but WD convergence clean, H5 metrics computable and differentiating. H2/H6/H7 not yet tested, no contradictions.

path_to_full_result.evidence: Clear phased plan. Codebase functional. All methods implemented. 117 GPU-hours feasible. Infrastructure validated.

novelty.evidence: Novelty score 7/10 from report. Phi-framework, WD Stability Condition, standardized metrics, compute-controlled benchmark all novel.

resource_efficiency.evidence: 117 GPU-hours moderate for NeurIPS. Phased approach allows early termination. Pilot cost ~2 GPU-hours.

pilot_signal_strength.evidence: Same pilot data supports contrarian thesis.

hypothesis_survival.evidence: Budget equivalence and CWD mechanism hypothesis have preliminary support.

path_to_full_result.evidence: Simpler scope but still needs ImageNet for credibility.

novelty.evidence: D'Angelo et al. NeurIPS 2024 already establishes WD-as-effective-LR narrative. Risks incremental label.

pilot_signal_strength.evidence: Not tested. Pilot shows WD variation has minimal effect, undermining premise of complex WD controller.

hypothesis_survival.evidence: Budget equivalence finding is actively hostile: if WD schedule doesn't matter, why build 3-level controller?

path_to_full_result.evidence: Requires complete redesign, many hyperparameters, >200 GPU-hours with high null-result risk.

novelty.evidence: Novelty 9/10. Zero arXiv results. Genuinely novel framing.

resource_efficiency.evidence: High cost for uncertain payoff. Many hyperparameters to tune.

H1_proximal_unification.evidence: Soft CWD effective WD converges across beta values (0.000239-0.000247). Accuracy gap between soft(beta=100) and hard CWD is -0.17%, within noise. Non-monotonic beta-accuracy relationship needs multi-seed resolution.
H1_proximal_unification.concern: Soft CWD accuracy convergence is noisy, not clean monotonic.

H2_stability_condition.evidence: No warmup sweep conducted in pilot. Scheduled for stability_experiment task.

H3_budget_equivalence.evidence: 4 methods with 10x WD budget differences (BEM 0.0-0.9) achieve 89.29-89.50% accuracy. 0.21% spread on single seed.

H4_cwd_mechanism.evidence: CWD (89.29%) does not outperform baseline (89.48%). CWD's mean_wd_actual (2.47e-4) is ~50% of nominal, confirming budget reduction mechanism. Falsification battery not yet run.

H5_metric_predictiveness.evidence: CSI differentiates methods (CV ~2%, range 0.853-0.897). AIS ranges 0.296-0.402. BEM clearly differentiates (0.0-0.9). Cross-validation not yet possible (single benchmark).

H6_spatial_superiority.evidence: Requires per-layer WD experiments and VGG architecture. Scheduled for spatial_vs_temporal task.

H7_composition_orthogonality.evidence: Requires CWD+Cosine and CWD+AdamWN experiments. Scheduled for composition_experiment task.

reasons: Budget equivalence strongly confirmed: 0.21% accuracy spread across 4 methods with 10x WD budget differences
CWD does NOT outperform AdamW constant baseline (89.29% vs 89.48%), supporting the framework's prediction
All three proposed metrics (CSI, AIS, BEM) are computable and differentiating -- BEM is especially informative
Codebase fully functional with all WD methods implemented and diagnostic logging validated
No core hypothesis falsified; H3 and H4 have positive preliminary evidence
Contrarian backup is strictly dominated (lower novelty, same empirical base)
Allostatic backup is actively contradicted by pilot evidence (WD variation has near-zero effect)
next_actions: Proceed to cifar10_resnet20_full: 7 primary methods + CWD ablations, 3 seeds, 200 epochs
Prioritize CWD falsification battery (effective-lambda-matched, random-mask, inverted-mask)
Run stability_experiment (H2 warmup sweep) in parallel with CIFAR full
Cross-validate metrics on CIFAR-100 before committing to ImageNet (72 GPU-hours)
Include both hard and soft (beta=100) CWD in full benchmark for multi-seed proximal approximation test
concerns: Soft CWD convergence is noisy (non-monotonic beta-accuracy). Multi-seed runs should resolve.
CSI has narrow spread (CV ~2%). May need refinement if it remains non-predictive across benchmarks.
AIS values are moderate (0.30-0.40), not low (<0.10) as hypothesized. Full CWD battery needed to disambiguate.
Single seed (42) only. Multi-seed experiments are critical for statistical confidence.
